<<Overview of the Accident>>  
<<Air France Flight 4590>> <<on July 25, 1994>> <<was a notable aviation accident>> <<that resulted in the loss of 113 lives>>. <<The aircraft>> <<was a Concorde>> <<which was traveling from Charles de Gaulle Airport in France to New York>>. <<The accident occurred during takeoff>> <<when a piece of metal from a Boeing 747>> <<struck the Concorde>> <<at a low altitude>>. <<This caused a fuel tank to rupture>> <<and a fire to break out>> <<which led to the crash of the aircraft>>. <<The energy released by the fire>> <<was catastrophic>> <<and the gap between the two aircraft was minimal>> <<making the collision inevitable>>. <<The tap of the metal fragment>> <<was the trigger>> <<for the disaster>>. *highlighted section*

---  
<<Investigation Process>>  
<<The investigation into the accident>> <<was led by the French Bureau of Inquiry and Analysis for Civil Aviation Safety>> <<and the European Aviation Safety Agency>>. <<The investigation focused on the gap between the two aircraft>> <<and the sequence of events that led to the collision>>. <<Experts examined the debris>> <<and the damage to the Concorde>> <<to determine the cause of the accident>>. <<They also reviewed the flight data recorders>> <<and the cockpit voice recorders>> <<to understand the pilots' actions>>. <<The investigation highlighted the importance of energy management in aviation>> <<and the need for better communication between airlines>>. <<The tap of the metal fragment>> <<was identified as the critical factor>> <<in the accident>>. *highlighted section*

<<Outcomes and Impact>>  
<<The outcomes of the investigation>> <<included a series of safety recommendations>> <<to prevent similar accidents in the future>>. <<The accident highlighted the gap in aviation safety protocols>> <<and the need for improved maintenance procedures>>. <<As a result>> <<new regulations were introduced>> <<regarding the inspection of aircraft>> <<and the handling of foreign objects on runways>>. <<The incident also had a significant impact on the aviation industry>> <<and led to a temporary grounding of Concorde flights>>. <<The energy of the collision>> <<was a key factor in the severity of the accident>> <<and
